ZEAL/BZea DTS GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05

  • Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • View Source

Days-to-silk sibling of the DTA notebook: the same SNP50K ancestry pipeline and flowering candidate set, with per-field SpATS days-to-silk BLUEs as the phenotype. Significance is Benjamini–Hochberg FDR ≤ 0.05 (per-scan q-values): the Manhattan line is the −log₁₀P at which BH q = 0.05, and candidate/peak calls report q.

Author

Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata

Published

July 9, 2026

What this is. The days-to-silk (DTS) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-dta-mlm-snp50k-fdr, built from the same real BZea pipeline: field DTS → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same 1,403 near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa (Zx 578, Zv 385, Zd 255, Zl 118, Zh 61). The DTS BLUE is the per-field SpATS mean across two fields (CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5); union mean ≈ 82 ± 4 days. DTS is a flowering trait, essentially the female counterpart of DTA (anthesis): the two are tightly genetically correlated, so the candidate genes and the ancestry-flowering confound below are identical to the DTA notebook — only the phenotype changes.

ImportantGenotype at 0.4× skim: why ancestry, not per-SNP

SNP50K is 51,991 teosinte-vs-B73 sites pileup-genotyped at ~0.4× — hard genotypes are unusable (70% missing, ~0% hom-ALT), so ancestry is inferred from the per-sample allele counts. The per-SNP genotype (bcftools gpHWE) (Panel B) is therefore λ_GC-deflated (sparse mean-imputation); the RTIGER ancestry mosaic (Panel C) is the usable, block-smoothed genotype (teosinte presence 0.111, matching the established BzeaSeq ~0.101). This is the SNP50K-specific reason ancestry inference is mandatory.

As in DTA, K = teosinte-ancestry relatedness is collinear with the flowering QTL, so the mixed model absorbs the very signal it protects — the flowering candidates (ZmCCT10, ZCN8) are suppressed under MLM. The DTS QTL are therefore the province of JLM (nested-in-family stepwise), not the MLM Manhattan.

Show code 
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code 
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/dts_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/dts_gwas_mlm_gphwe_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/dts_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/dts_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
ALPHA <- 0.05
fdr_lod <- function(p, alpha = ALPHA) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0]
  q <- p.adjust(p, "BH")
  if (!any(q <= alpha)) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }
  -log10(max(p[q <= alpha]))
}
qval <- function(p) p.adjust(p, "BH") # BH q-values aligned to p
NoteSignificance = Benjamini–Hochberg FDR ≤ 0.05

Every Manhattan’s dotted line is drawn at the −log₁₀P where BH q = 0.05 for that scan; a marker is significant iff its BH q ≤ 0.05. If no marker passes FDR, no line is drawn. Candidate/peak tables report q.

Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker

For each maize flowering candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). The same v5 flowering candidates as DTA (these are maize genes; teosinte introgression at them drives flowering time in both sexes).

Show code 
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)][, q := qval(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/dts_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("DTS(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], QTL = gg$qtl[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `window-max q (BH)` = if (nrow(w)) signif(w$q[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `FDR<=0.05` = if (nrow(w)) w$q[kw] <= ALPHA else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = "DTS candidates (B73 v5): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan, with Benjamini–Hochberg q at the window-max marker."
)
DTS candidates (B73 v5): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan, with Benjamini–Hochberg q at the window-max marker.
gene QTL chr gene start (v5) closest Δstart (bp) closest -log10P window-max -log10P window-max q (BH) FDR<=0.05
zagl1 DTS(chr1) 1 283,849,414 12,915 0.67 0.84 0.790 FALSE
zmads69 DTS(chr3) 3 161,172,047 29,298 0.28 0.48 0.920 FALSE
zcn12 DTA3.2 3 202,794,887 3,509 0.68 0.85 0.790 FALSE
tu1 DTA4.1 4 183,877,026 -2,231 0.84 0.84 0.790 FALSE
zmm31 DTA5.1 5 7,374,462 -50,600 0.43 0.73 0.840 FALSE
si1 DTA6.1 6 94,458,037 12,722 0.68 0.68 0.860 FALSE
zag1 DTA6.2 6 142,692,209 65,043 0.97 1.26 0.580 FALSE
dlf1 DTA7.2 7 184,528,042 -547,303 1.23 NA NA NA
zcn8 DTS(chr8) 8 126,678,681 -57,791 0.28 0.31 0.970 FALSE
zmcct9 DTS(chr9) 9 120,960,326 7,897 0.30 0.37 0.960 FALSE
zmcct10 DTS(chr10) 10 96,175,873 -2,308 1.66 3.12 0.028 TRUE

OLS → MLM: the flowering confound and structure control

The fixed-effect OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Taxon + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD); K deflates λ toward 1 and collapses the flowering peaks (ZmCCT10), the ancestry-QTL confound.

Show code 
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea DTS — OLS (Taxon + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = FDR 0.05)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(ols$P),
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/dts_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)
Show code 
lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Taxon + marker, no K)", "MLM (Taxon + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  `n FDR<=0.05` = c(sum(qval(ols$P) <= ALPHA), sum(qval(mlm$P) <= ALPHA)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers at BH FDR ≤ 0.05.")
OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers at BH FDR ≤ 0.05.
model markers lambda_GC n FDR<=0.05 max_logP
OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) 48900 2.68 9911 6.88
MLM (Taxon + K) 48900 1.06 1428 13.74
Show code 
ov <- fread(overlap)
peak_at <- function(scan, ch, s) {
  w <- scan[CHR == ch & abs(BP - s) <= 5e5 & is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  if (!nrow(w)) NA_real_ else round(max(-log10(w$P)), 2)
}
cmp <- ov[, .(
  gene = symbol, chr,
  `OLS peak` = mapply(peak_at, list(ols), chr, start),
  `MLM peak` = mapply(peak_at, list(mlm), chr, start)
)]
cmp[, `drop (OLS−MLM)` := round(`OLS peak` - `MLM peak`, 2)]
setorder(cmp, -`OLS peak`)
knitr::kable(cmp, caption = "Local peak -log10P (±500 kb) per candidate: OLS vs MLM. K suppresses the ancestry-collinear flowering peaks.")
Local peak -log10P (±500 kb) per candidate: OLS vs MLM. K suppresses the ancestry-collinear flowering peaks.
gene chr OLS peak MLM peak drop (OLS−MLM)
dlf1 7 NA NA NA
zmcct10 10 6.14 3.12 3.02
tu1 4 2.18 0.84 1.34
zmm31 5 1.55 0.73 0.82
si1 6 1.21 0.68 0.53
zmads69 3 1.09 0.48 0.61
zagl1 1 0.47 0.84 -0.37
zcn8 8 0.41 0.31 0.10
zag1 6 0.26 1.26 -1.00
zmcct9 9 0.15 0.37 -0.22
zcn12 3 0.13 0.85 -0.72

JLM QTL genomic distribution (Panel A)

DTS QTL from JLM (TASSEL StepwiseOLS, nested in taxon, enter/exit P=1e-4=LOD4). Model-independent — the primary flowering analysis, as in Chen 2019.

Show code 
p_lolli <- plot_lollipop(
  here("results/sim/zeal/tassel/dts_jlm_native1.txt"), mlm,
  "ZEAL/BZea DTS — JLM QTL genomic distribution (nested in taxon)",
  overlap_csv = overlap, label_genes = c("zcn8", "zcn12", "zmcct9", "zmcct10", "dlf1", "tu1"),
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/dts_jlm_lollipop_snp50k.png")
)
p_lolli

MLM (Taxon + K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (Panel C)

Show code 
p_mlm <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea DTS — MLM (Taxon + K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = FDR 0.05)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(mlm$P), out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/dts_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)
p_mlm
Show code 
cat(sprintf("%d markers pass FDR <= 0.05 (BH); FDR line at -log10P = %s\n", sum(qval(mlm$P) <= ALPHA), ifelse(is.na(fdr_lod(mlm$P)), "NA (none)", sprintf("%.2f", fdr_lod(mlm$P)))))
1428 markers pass FDR <= 0.05 (BH); FDR line at -log10P = 2.84

Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)

Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools gpHWE)) is λ_GC-deflated but catches sharp SNP hits (ZCN8) the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype. The B↔︎C contrast is the notebook’s per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing.

Show code 
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("DTS — MLM (Taxon+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools gpHWE) (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(gt$P)
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("DTS — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(mlm$P)
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/dts_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite

Multi-caller DTS scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)

The same DTS MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth. Caller reliability at low coverage: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER per-donor segments; binhmm 1 Mb bins) recover the correct teosinte fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the fresh per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call and give λ-deflated scans. Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (DTS) changes.

Show code 
callers <- c(control = "gphwe_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
tb1 <- fread(here("data/teonam/dts_candidate_genes.tsv"))[symbol == "zmcct10"]
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/dts_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  z <- sc[CHR == tb1$chr & abs(BP - tb1$start) <= 5e5]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n FDR<=0.05` = sum(qval(sc$P) <= ALPHA),
    `zmcct10 q (BH)` = if (nrow(z)) signif(min(qval(sc$P)[sc$CHR == tb1$chr & abs(sc$BP - tb1$start) <= 5e5]), 2) else NA_real_
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "DTS MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers at BH FDR ≤ 0.05 and the zmcct10 q. Aggregated callers (RTIGER, binhmm) recover teosinte presence ~0.10; per-SNP (control) and per-SNP recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call and deflate λ_GC.")
DTS MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers at BH FDR ≤ 0.05 and the zmcct10 q. Aggregated callers (RTIGER, binhmm) recover teosinte presence ~0.10; per-SNP (control) and per-SNP recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call and deflate λ_GC.
caller teosinte presence lambda_GC n FDR<=0.05 zmcct10 q (BH)
control 0.008 0.68 477 0.8200
nNIL 0.018 0.80 173 0.0085
RTIGER 0.111 1.06 1428 0.0280
LB-Impute 0.035 0.62 313 0.3900
binhmm 0.101 0.84 1138 0.0077
Show code 
panels <- lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/dts_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", callers[[nm]])))
  plot_manhattan(sc, sprintf("DTS MLM (Taxon+K) — %s (λ_GC = %.2f; FDR 0.05)", nm, lam(sc$P)), overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(sc$P))
})
cc <- cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = panels, ncol = 1, labels = LETTERS[1:5], label_size = 18, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/dts_caller_composite_fdr_snp50k.png"), cc, width = 9, height = 20, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
cc
Source Code
---
title: "ZEAL/BZea DTS GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05"
subtitle: "Days-to-silk sibling of the DTA notebook: the same SNP50K ancestry pipeline and flowering candidate set, with per-field SpATS days-to-silk BLUEs as the phenotype. Significance is Benjamini–Hochberg FDR ≤ 0.05 (per-scan q-values): the Manhattan line is the −log₁₀P at which BH q = 0.05, and candidate/peak calls report q."
author: "Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false

---

**What this is.** The days-to-silk (DTS) sibling of
[`zeal-qtl-recovery-dta-mlm-snp50k-fdr`](zeal-qtl-recovery-dta-mlm-snp50k-fdr.html), built from the
same real BZea pipeline: field DTS → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage →
RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same 1,403 near-isogenic
B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from **5 teosinte taxa** (Zx 578, Zv 385, Zd 255, Zl 118, Zh 61). The
DTS BLUE is the per-field SpATS mean across two fields (CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5); union mean ≈ 82 ± 4
days. DTS is a **flowering** trait, essentially the female counterpart of DTA (anthesis): the two
are tightly genetically correlated, so the candidate genes and the ancestry-flowering confound
below are identical to the DTA notebook — only the phenotype changes.

::: {.callout-important}
## Genotype at 0.4× skim: why ancestry, not per-SNP
SNP50K is 51,991 teosinte-vs-B73 sites pileup-genotyped at ~0.4× — hard genotypes are
unusable (70% missing, ~0% hom-ALT), so ancestry is inferred from the per-sample allele
**counts**. The **per-SNP genotype (bcftools gpHWE) (Panel B)** is therefore λ_GC-deflated (sparse
mean-imputation); the **RTIGER ancestry mosaic (Panel C)** is the usable, block-smoothed
genotype (teosinte presence 0.111, matching the established BzeaSeq ~0.101). This is the
SNP50K-specific reason ancestry inference is mandatory.
:::

As in DTA, **K = teosinte-ancestry relatedness is collinear with the flowering QTL**, so the mixed
model absorbs the very signal it protects — the flowering candidates (`ZmCCT10`, `ZCN8`) are
suppressed under MLM. The DTS QTL are therefore the province of **JLM** (nested-in-family
stepwise), not the MLM Manhattan.

```{r setup}
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
```

```{r load}
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/dts_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/dts_gwas_mlm_gphwe_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/dts_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/dts_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
ALPHA <- 0.05
fdr_lod <- function(p, alpha = ALPHA) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0]
  q <- p.adjust(p, "BH")
  if (!any(q <= alpha)) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }
  -log10(max(p[q <= alpha]))
}
qval <- function(p) p.adjust(p, "BH") # BH q-values aligned to p
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Significance = Benjamini–Hochberg FDR ≤ 0.05
Every Manhattan's dotted line is drawn at the **−log₁₀P where BH q = 0.05** for that scan; a
marker is significant iff its BH q ≤ 0.05. If no marker passes FDR, no line is drawn.
Candidate/peak tables report q.
:::

## Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker

For each maize flowering candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the
highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic **MLM (Taxon+K)** scan (Panel C).
The same v5 flowering candidates as DTA (these are maize genes; teosinte introgression at them
drives flowering time in both sexes).

```{r candidate-markers}
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)][, q := qval(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/dts_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("DTS(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], QTL = gg$qtl[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `window-max q (BH)` = if (nrow(w)) signif(w$q[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `FDR<=0.05` = if (nrow(w)) w$q[kw] <= ALPHA else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = "DTS candidates (B73 v5): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan, with Benjamini–Hochberg q at the window-max marker."
)
```

## OLS → MLM: the flowering confound and structure control

The fixed-effect OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Taxon + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same
fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD); K deflates λ toward 1 **and**
collapses the flowering peaks (`ZmCCT10`), the ancestry-QTL confound.

```{r ols-manhattan, fig.width=9, fig.height=4.4}
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea DTS — OLS (Taxon + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = FDR 0.05)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(ols$P),
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/dts_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)
```

```{r ols-vs-mlm}
lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Taxon + marker, no K)", "MLM (Taxon + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  `n FDR<=0.05` = c(sum(qval(ols$P) <= ALPHA), sum(qval(mlm$P) <= ALPHA)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers at BH FDR ≤ 0.05.")
```

```{r ols-vs-mlm-peaks}
ov <- fread(overlap)
peak_at <- function(scan, ch, s) {
  w <- scan[CHR == ch & abs(BP - s) <= 5e5 & is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  if (!nrow(w)) NA_real_ else round(max(-log10(w$P)), 2)
}
cmp <- ov[, .(
  gene = symbol, chr,
  `OLS peak` = mapply(peak_at, list(ols), chr, start),
  `MLM peak` = mapply(peak_at, list(mlm), chr, start)
)]
cmp[, `drop (OLS−MLM)` := round(`OLS peak` - `MLM peak`, 2)]
setorder(cmp, -`OLS peak`)
knitr::kable(cmp, caption = "Local peak -log10P (±500 kb) per candidate: OLS vs MLM. K suppresses the ancestry-collinear flowering peaks.")
```

## JLM QTL genomic distribution (Panel A)

DTS QTL from JLM (TASSEL StepwiseOLS, nested in taxon, enter/exit P=1e-4=LOD4). Model-independent
— the primary flowering analysis, as in Chen 2019.

```{r lollipop-jlm, fig.width=9, fig.height=4}
p_lolli <- plot_lollipop(
  here("results/sim/zeal/tassel/dts_jlm_native1.txt"), mlm,
  "ZEAL/BZea DTS — JLM QTL genomic distribution (nested in taxon)",
  overlap_csv = overlap, label_genes = c("zcn8", "zcn12", "zmcct9", "zmcct10", "dlf1", "tu1"),
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/dts_jlm_lollipop_snp50k.png")
)
p_lolli
```

## MLM (Taxon + K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (Panel C)

```{r manhattan-mlm, fig.width=9, fig.height=4.4}
p_mlm <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea DTS — MLM (Taxon + K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = FDR 0.05)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(mlm$P), out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/dts_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)
p_mlm
cat(sprintf("%d markers pass FDR <= 0.05 (BH); FDR line at -log10P = %s\n", sum(qval(mlm$P) <= ALPHA), ifelse(is.na(fdr_lod(mlm$P)), "NA (none)", sprintf("%.2f", fdr_lod(mlm$P)))))
```

## Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)

Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools gpHWE)) is λ_GC-deflated but catches sharp SNP hits (`ZCN8`) the
mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated
genotype. The B↔C contrast is the notebook's per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing.

```{r composite, fig.width=9, fig.height=13}
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("DTS — MLM (Taxon+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools gpHWE) (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(gt$P)
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("DTS — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(mlm$P)
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/dts_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite
```

## Multi-caller DTS scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)

The same DTS MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller's recovered genotypes, at the real
~0.4× depth. Caller reliability at low coverage: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER
per-donor segments; binhmm 1 Mb bins) recover the correct teosinte fraction (~0.10), while
per-SNP calling (control) and the fresh per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) **under-call** and
give λ-deflated scans. Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the
phenotype (DTS) changes.

```{r caller-table}
callers <- c(control = "gphwe_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
tb1 <- fread(here("data/teonam/dts_candidate_genes.tsv"))[symbol == "zmcct10"]
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/dts_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  z <- sc[CHR == tb1$chr & abs(BP - tb1$start) <= 5e5]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n FDR<=0.05` = sum(qval(sc$P) <= ALPHA),
    `zmcct10 q (BH)` = if (nrow(z)) signif(min(qval(sc$P)[sc$CHR == tb1$chr & abs(sc$BP - tb1$start) <= 5e5]), 2) else NA_real_
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "DTS MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers at BH FDR ≤ 0.05 and the zmcct10 q. Aggregated callers (RTIGER, binhmm) recover teosinte presence ~0.10; per-SNP (control) and per-SNP recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call and deflate λ_GC.")
```

```{r caller-composite, fig.width=9, fig.height=20}
panels <- lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/dts_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", callers[[nm]])))
  plot_manhattan(sc, sprintf("DTS MLM (Taxon+K) — %s (λ_GC = %.2f; FDR 0.05)", nm, lam(sc$P)), overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(sc$P))
})
cc <- cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = panels, ncol = 1, labels = LETTERS[1:5], label_size = 18, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/dts_caller_composite_fdr_snp50k.png"), cc, width = 9, height = 20, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
cc
```
